title: 10 best herbal teas for jaundice
intro_desc: Herbal teas for jaundice are infusions made from plants and herbs that can help alleviate the symptoms of jaundice, a condition characterized by the yellowing of the skin and eyes due to an accumulation of bilirubin in the body. These teas can support liver function, promote bile flow, and help eliminate toxins from the body. Examples of herbal teas that can be beneficial for jaundice include dandelion root, milk thistle, and chicory. For instance, dandelion root tea can stimulate bile production and flow, while milk thistle tea can protect and repair liver cells. By improving liver function, these teas can help reduce bilirubin levels in the body, thereby alleviating jaundice symptoms and improving overall health and well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tea helps with jaundice because it promotes healthy liver function, which is crucial for managing this condition. Jaundice occurs when there is an excess of bilirubin in the blood, often due to liver issues. Dandelion is known for its ability to stimulate bile production and support the liver's detoxification process. This can help the liver effectively metabolize bilirubin, reducing its levels in the bloodstream and alleviating jaundice symptoms. Additionally, dandelion tea contains antioxidants that protect liver cells from damage, further supporting liver health and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Diuretic: Dandelion tea increases urine production, which helps remove excess bilirubin from the body, one of the main causes of jaundice. This property is attributed to the terpenoid content and taraxacin.

Cholagogue: It promotes the flow of bile from the liver, aiding in the elimination of waste and reducing the risk of gallstones, a common complication of jaundice. This is primarily due to the taraxasterol and taraxacin constituents.

Hepatoprotective: Dandelion tea protects the liver from damage, supports its function, and helps in regeneration. This is attributed to its antioxidant properties, vitamins, and minerals, including vitamin C, vitamin B, potassium, and iron.

Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion tea reduces inflammation in the liver and bile ducts, alleviating jaundice symptoms. This property is attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, especially taraxacin and taraxasterol.

Antioxidant: Dandelion tea neutralizes free radicals and prevents oxidative stress in liver cells, promoting overall liver health. This is due to its high content of phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and carotenoids.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 tablespoons of fresh dandelion flowers and leaves, or 1-2 teaspoons of dried dandelion herb.
Rinse the fresh dandelion parts under cold water to remove dirt and insects.
Place the dandelion parts in a pot and add 2 cups of water; bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the spent dandelion parts.
Drink the tea while warm, 2-3 times a day, for several days to help treat jaundice.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of dandelions are used to make medicinal teas for jaundice due to their ability to promote the flow of bile from the liver.
Leaves: Dandelion leaves are rich in vitamins and minerals, making them a valuable addition to teas for jaundice treatment.
Flowers: Dandelion flowers contain antioxidants that help protect liver cells from damage, making them useful in teas for jaundice.
Buds: The buds of dandelions are a great source of vitamins and minerals, and are used in teas to support liver function in jaundice.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle tea helps with jaundice because it contains silymarin, a flavonoid that has been shown to have protective effects on the liver. Jaundice is a condition characterized by the yellowing of the skin and eyes, which occurs when there is an excess of bilirubin in the blood. Bilirubin is a waste product that is produced when the liver breaks down old red blood cells. Milk thistle tea may help to reduce the levels of bilirubin in the blood by supporting the liver's ability to process and eliminate it. Additionally, milk thistle has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, further supporting its function and helping to alleviate jaundice.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Milk thistle tea can reduce inflammation in the liver, which is important for jaundice as it helps to alleviate damage to liver cells and promotes healing. This property is due to the active constituent, silymarin.

Hepatoprotective: Milk thistle tea has liver-protecting properties, which is crucial for jaundice as it helps to prevent further liver damage and promotes the regeneration of liver cells. The active constituent responsible for this is silymarin.

Antioxidant: Milk thistle tea has antioxidant properties that help to protect liver cells from free radical damage, which is important for jaundice as it reduces oxidative stress in the liver. Silymarin is the active constituent that provides this antioxidant effect.

Cholagogue: Milk thistle tea stimulates the flow of bile from the liver, which is important for jaundice as it helps to eliminate bilirubin from the body. The active constituent responsible for this property is silymarin.

Detoxification: Milk thistle tea supports the detoxification process in the liver, which is crucial for jaundice as it helps to eliminate toxins that can cause liver damage. The active constituent responsible for this is silymarin.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top pot, pour 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried milk thistle seeds, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the seeds, then let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 2-3 cups of milk thistle tea daily, between meals, for a few weeks to help treat jaundice.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of the milk thistle plant are the most commonly used part for making medicinal teas for jaundice due to their high concentration of silymarin, a flavonoid that has been shown to have liver-protecting properties.

Leaves: The leaves of the milk thistle plant are also used in medicinal teas for jaundice, as they contain silymarin and other beneficial compounds that can help support liver function and promote bile flow.

Flowers: The flowers of the milk thistle plant are used less frequently than the seeds and leaves, but they do contain some silymarin and other bioactive compounds that can support liver health and help reduce inflammation associated with jaundice.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tea helps with jaundice because it contains curcumin, a powerful antioxidant that aids in liver function and detoxification. The liver plays a crucial role in managing jaundice, as it is responsible for breaking down bilirubin, a yellow pigment that accumulates in the blood and tissues, causing the characteristic yellowing of the skin and eyes. By promoting liver health and facilitating bilirubin metabolism, turmeric tea can help alleviate jaundice symptoms. Furthermore, its anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the liver, enhancing overall liver function and supporting the body's natural healing process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Curcumin, the main active ingredient in turmeric, helps reduce inflammation in the liver, which is beneficial for jaundice as it often involves liver inflammation or damage.
Antioxidant: Turmeric contains powerful antioxidants that protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals, supporting liver function and aiding in jaundice recovery.
Hepatoprotective: Turmeric has been shown to have hepatoprotective properties, helping to protect the liver from toxins and promoting its overall health, which is crucial for jaundice treatment.
Cholagogue: Turmeric stimulates bile production and flow, helping the liver to eliminate bilirubin, a primary cause of jaundice, from the body.
Antibacterial: The antibacterial properties of turmeric can help prevent infections that may occur as a result of jaundice, promoting overall well-being and supporting recovery.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water with 1 teaspoon of dried turmeric root for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove the turmeric pieces, then add the juice of half a lemon and 1 teaspoon of honey for taste.
Warm up the mixture again on low heat until it's comfortably hot.
Drink the tea in small sips, aiming to finish it within 30-45 minutes.
Repeat this process 2-3 times a day for best results in managing jaundice symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric rhizomes are commonly used to make medicinal teas for jaundice due to their high curcumin content, which has been shown to help reduce bilirubin levels.
Leaves: Turmeric leaves have been used in traditional medicine to treat jaundice, as they contain curcumin and other compounds that may help stimulate bile production and promote liver function.
Flowers: Turmeric flowers are used less frequently than rhizomes or leaves, but they do contain curcumin and other bioactive compounds that may support liver health and help reduce jaundice symptoms.

Seeds: Turmeric seeds are not as commonly used as other parts of the plant, but they do contain curcumin and other beneficial compounds that may help support liver function and reduce jaundice symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common barberry tea helps with jaundice because it contains berberine, an alkaloid that has been shown to have a positive effect on liver function. Jaundice is a condition characterized by the yellowing of the skin and eyes due to the buildup of bilirubin in the body. The liver plays a crucial role in processing bilirubin, and barberry tea can help support this process. Berberine has been shown to stimulate the production of bile, which helps the liver remove bilirubin from the body. Additionally, barberry tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the liver, further supporting its function and helping to alleviate jaundice symptoms.
remedy_properties: Cholagogue: Barberry tea stimulates the flow of bile from the liver, which is important for jaundice as it helps eliminate bilirubin, a pigment that accumulates in the blood in jaundice. This property is attributed to the alkaloid berberine.

Hepatoprotective: Barberry tea protects the liver from damage, which is crucial in jaundice as the condition is often associated with liver dysfunction. The hepatoprotective effect is attributed to the antioxidant properties of berberine and other flavonoids present in barberry.

Anti-inflammatory: Barberry tea reduces inflammation, which is important for jaundice as inflammation can exacerbate liver damage. The anti-inflammatory effect is attributed to berberine and other plant compounds in barberry.

Antimicrobial: Barberry tea inhibits the growth of bacteria, which is crucial in jaundice as it can be caused by bacterial infections. The antimicrobial effect is attributed to berberine, which has been shown to be effective against a variety of bacteria.

Diuretic: Barberry tea increases urine production, which helps eliminate toxins from the body, including bilirubin. This property is attributed to the alkaloid berberine and other compounds in barberry.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 2 teaspoons of dried barberry bark to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solid remains.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain berberine, which helps to improve liver function and reduce jaundice symptoms.
Bark: Rich in alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and liver-protecting properties, making it useful for jaundice treatment.
Leaves: Contain tannins and flavonoids, which have astringent and anti-oxidant properties that help in reducing jaundice symptoms.
Flowers: Have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help in soothing liver inflammation and supporting bile production, thus beneficial for jaundice.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tea helps with jaundice because it has natural detoxifying properties that can support liver function. The root of the burdock plant contains active compounds that may stimulate bile production and promote the flow of bile from the liver, which can help to eliminate waste and toxins from the body. This, in turn, can help to reduce the symptoms of jaundice, such as yellowing of the skin and eyes, caused by the buildup of bilirubin in the body. Additionally, burdock tea has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help to protect liver cells from damage and support overall liver health. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using burdock tea or any other herbal remedy to treat jaundice or any other medical condition.
remedy_properties: Diuretic: Burdock tea increases urine production, which helps eliminate bilirubin from the body, a key factor in jaundice treatment. This is mainly due to the polyacetylenes and tannins present in burdock.
Anti-inflammatory: Burdock tea reduces inflammation in the liver, promoting healing and reducing jaundice symptoms. This is attributed to its essential oil and polyacetylenes content.

Antioxidant: Burdock tea neutralizes free radicals, protecting liver cells from damage and supporting liver function during jaundice. The antioxidant properties come from its phenolic acids, flavonoids, and quercetin.

Blood purification: Burdock tea helps cleanse the blood by removing toxins, which can help reduce the workload on the liver and speed up jaundice recovery. The blood-purifying effect is primarily due to its arctigenin and lignans content.

Choleretic: Burdock tea stimulates bile production in the liver, aiding in the digestion of fats and the elimination of waste products, which is beneficial for jaundice treatment. The choleretic effect is attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and polyacetylenes.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried burdock root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the burdock root pieces.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 2-3 cups of the burdock tea daily for several weeks to help treat jaundice.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots are commonly used in medicinal teas for jaundice due to their ability to promote blood purification and eliminate toxins from the body.
Seeds: Burdock seeds are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them helpful in treating jaundice by reducing liver inflammation.
Leaves: Burdock leaves contain active compounds that support liver function and bile production, which can help alleviate symptoms of jaundice.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Greater celandine tea helps with jaundice because it contains alkaloids, such as chelidonine, that have been shown to stimulate bile production and flow, which is essential in the treatment of jaundice. This condition is characterized by the yellowing of the skin and eyes due to the accumulation of bilirubin in the body. By promoting the excretion of bile from the liver, greater celandine tea aids in the elimination of bilirubin, thus alleviating the symptoms of jaundice. Furthermore, its anti-inflammatory and hepatoprotective properties help protect and repair liver cells, enhancing overall liver function.
remedy_properties: Cholagogue action: Greater celandine tea stimulates the flow of bile from the liver, which is important for jaundice as it helps eliminate bilirubin, a primary cause of the condition. The active constituents responsible for this property are alkaloids such as chelidonine, berberine, and sanguinarine.

Hepatoprotective properties: Greater celandine tea protects the liver, which is crucial for jaundice as the condition is often associated with liver damage or dysfunction. The hepatoprotective effects are attributed to the alkaloids and flavonoids present in the tea.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Greater celandine tea reduces inflammation, which is important for jaundice as inflammation can exacerbate liver damage. The anti-inflammatory properties are primarily due to the alkaloids chelidonine and berberine.

Antioxidant properties: Greater celandine tea contains antioxidants, which help protect liver cells from oxidative stress. This property is important for jaundice as oxidative stress can contribute to liver damage and impaired bile flow. The antioxidants in greater celandine tea include various flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Mild diuretic properties: Greater celandine tea promotes diuresis, which aids in the elimination of toxins from the body. This property is important for jaundice as it helps remove bilirubin and other waste products that can accumulate due to liver dysfunction. The diuretic effects of greater celandine tea are attributed to its alkaloids and saponins.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried greater celandine herb, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat, let it simmer for 10-15 minutes, and maintain a gentle bubbling.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the used herbs.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 2-3 cups of this herbal tea daily, between meals, for several weeks to help treat jaundice.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the greater celandine plant are used to make medicinal teas for jaundice due to their anti-inflammatory and choleretic properties, which help to stimulate the flow of bile and promote liver function.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the greater celandine plant are used in medicinal teas for jaundice due to their alkaloid content, which has been shown to have a positive effect on liver health and bile production.

Flowers: The flowers of the greater celandine plant are used in medicinal teas for jaundice due to their high concentration of antioxidants, which help to protect liver cells from damage and support liver function.

Seeds: The seeds of the greater celandine plant are used in medicinal teas for jaundice due to their ability to stimulate bile production and promote the flow of bile from the liver, which can help to reduce the symptoms of jaundice.

Roots: The roots of the greater celandine plant are used in medicinal teas for jaundice due to their high content of alkaloids, which have been shown to have a positive effect on liver health and function, and can help to reduce the symptoms of jaundice.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yardweed tea helps with jaundice because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that support liver function. The tea is made from the leaves of the Chrysanthemum leucanthemum plant, which has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various liver ailments. The tea's active compounds, including flavonoids and terpenoids, help to protect liver cells from damage and promote the flow of bile. This can help to reduce the buildup of bilirubin in the body, which is a primary cause of jaundice. Additionally, yardweed tea has been shown to improve liver enzyme levels and reduce inflammation in the liver, further supporting its use as a natural remedy for jaundice.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yardweed tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the liver, a common cause of jaundice, due to the presence of flavonoids like luteolin and apigenin.
Hepatoprotective: Yardweed tea protects liver cells from damage, helping to treat jaundice, thanks to its antioxidant properties and active constituents like silymarin.
Diuretic: Yardweed tea promotes diuresis, helping the body eliminate toxins and bilirubin, a primary cause of jaundice, due to its diuretic compounds like potassium and magnesium.
Choleretic: Yardweed tea stimulates bile production, aiding in the elimination of bilirubin, by containing active constituents like taraxasterol and ursolic acid.

Antioxidant: Yardweed tea's antioxidant properties help protect liver cells from oxidative stress and support liver function during jaundice treatment, due to its high concentration of phenolic acids, flavonoids, and tannins.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 fresh yardweed (also known as purslane) leaves and rinse them thoroughly to remove dirt and bacteria.
Crush or chop the leaves finely to release their juices and increase the surface area for extraction.
Place the crushed leaves in a cup and add boiling water, ensuring the leaves are fully submerged.
Cover the cup and let it steep for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea into another cup, discarding the leaves, and consume it while it's still warm, 2-3 times a day for best results.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, the leaves are used to make medicinal teas for jaundice due to their ability to promote liver function.
Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, the flowers are used in teas to help reduce liver inflammation associated with jaundice.
Seeds: High in essential oils, the seeds are used in medicinal teas for jaundice to stimulate bile production and support overall liver health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with jaundice because it contains flavonoids and coumarins, which are known to have hepatoprotective properties that can protect the liver from damage. The tea stimulates the production of bile, which aids in the elimination of waste from the body and can help to reduce the bilirubin levels in the blood, a key factor in jaundice. Additionally, licorice tea has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, further supporting its function and helping to alleviate jaundice symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of glycyrrhizin, help reduce inflammation in the liver, a common symptom of jaundice.
Hepatoprotective: Licorice tea's hepatoprotective properties, attributed to flavonoids like liquiritin, help protect the liver from damage and promote healing.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of licorice tea, provided by phenolic compounds, help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the liver.
Choleretic: Licorice tea's choleretic properties, due to the presence of glycyrrhetinic acid, help stimulate the production of bile, aiding in the elimination of bilirubin.

Immune-boosting: Licorice tea's immune-boosting properties, resulting from various polysaccharides, help strengthen the immune system, which is crucial for fighting off infections and supporting liver function during jaundice.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried licorice root, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the licorice root pieces.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 2-3 cups of the licorice tea daily, as part of a jaundice treatment plan.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English ivy tea helps with jaundice because it contains bioactive compounds that have hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds can help to protect the liver from damage and reduce inflammation, which can be beneficial for individuals with jaundice. Jaundice is a condition characterized by the yellowing of the skin and eyes, and it can be a sign of liver dysfunction. The liver plays a crucial role in breaking down bilirubin, a yellow pigment that is produced when old red blood cells are broken down. When the liver is not functioning properly, bilirubin can build up in the body, leading to jaundice. By supporting liver function and reducing inflammation, English ivy tea may help to alleviate the symptoms of jaundice.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English ivy tea can help reduce inflammation in the liver, which is important for jaundice as it can help alleviate one of the main symptoms, which is liver inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property are saponins and flavonoids.

Cholagogue: English ivy tea can help stimulate the production of bile, which is important for jaundice as it can help eliminate the excess bilirubin that causes the yellowing of the skin and eyes. The active constituent responsible for this property is hederagenin.

Hepatoprotective: English ivy tea can help protect the liver from damage, which is important for jaundice as it can help prevent further progression of the condition. The active constituents responsible for this property are saponins and flavonoids.

Diuretic: English ivy tea can help increase urine production, which is important for jaundice as it can help eliminate the excess bilirubin from the body. The active constituent responsible for this property is rutin.

Antioxidant: English ivy tea can help neutralize free radicals, which is important for jaundice as it can help reduce oxidative stress in the liver and promote healing. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids and tannins.
remedy_recipe: Pick 2-3 English ivy leaves, ensuring they are fully grown and free from pesticides.
Rinse the leaves thoroughly under cold water, then chop them into small pieces.
Place the chopped leaves in a pot and add 2 cups of water; bring to a boil, then simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves, and let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 1/2 cup of the English ivy tea, three times a day, for up to one week, while monitoring jaundice symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the English ivy plant are commonly used to make medicinal teas for jaundice due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to support liver function.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the English ivy plant are used in medicinal teas for jaundice because of their hepatoprotective properties, which can help to protect the liver from damage.
Stems: The stems of the English ivy plant are included in medicinal teas for jaundice due to their ability to stimulate bile production and promote liver function.
Flowers: The flowers of the English ivy plant are used in medicinal teas for jaundice because of their diuretic properties, which can help to eliminate toxins from the body.
Roots: The roots of the English ivy plant are used in medicinal teas for jaundice because of their cholagogue properties, which can help to stimulate the flow of bile from the liver.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tea helps with jaundice because it contains hypericin and hyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds may help to protect the liver and promote bile flow, which can be beneficial in managing jaundice. Additionally, St. John's wort tea has been shown to increase the activity of certain liver enzymes, which may help to break down bilirubin, a compound that can accumulate in the blood and cause jaundice. However, it is important to note that St. John's wort can interact with certain medications, so it is always best to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedies.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the liver, which is important for jaundice as it alleviates the symptoms caused by liver inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property are hypericin and hyperforin.

Hepatoprotective: St. John's wort tea has hepatoprotective properties that help protect the liver from damage, which is crucial for jaundice since it is often caused by liver issues. The active constituents responsible for this property are hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: St. John's wort tea has antioxidant properties that help protect liver cells from oxidative stress, which is important for jaundice as it prevents further damage to the liver. The active constituents responsible for this property are hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.

Choleretic: St. John's wort tea has choleretic properties that help stimulate the production of bile, which is important for jaundice as it helps eliminate bilirubin from the body. The active constituents responsible for this property are hypericin, hyperforin, and essential oils.

Antidepressant: St. John's wort tea has antidepressant properties that help alleviate anxiety and depression, which are common in jaundice patients due to the stress and discomfort caused by the condition. The active constituents responsible for this property are hypericin and hyperforin.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried St. John's Wort leaves.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, then let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 2-3 cups of the St. John's Wort tea daily, between meals, for jaundice treatment.
Continue this regimen for several weeks, or as directed by a healthcare professional.
remedy_parts: Flowers: St. John's Wort flowers are rich in hypericin and hyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help support liver function and alleviate jaundice symptoms.

Leaves: The leaves of St. John's Wort contain beneficial compounds, including hypericin and flavonoids, which may help stimulate bile production and support liver health, aiding in jaundice treatment.

Aerial parts: While not a specific part, aerial parts (stems and leaves) of St. John's Wort are often used together in teas for jaundice due to their combined liver-supporting and anti-inflammatory properties.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jaundice with herbal teas is by consuming infusions made from milk thistle and dandelion root. These herbs are known to support liver function, which is crucial in treating jaundice. Milk thistle contains silymarin, a potent antioxidant that protects liver cells from damage. Dandelion root, on the other hand, promotes bile production and flow, aiding in the elimination of bilirubin. Always consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaundice that are treated with herbal teas include liver disorders and gallbladder problems. Dandelion tea is known to promote liver health and bile production, helping to break down fats and reduce inflammation. Milk thistle tea is also beneficial for liver health, as it contains silymarin, an antioxidant that protects liver cells from damage. Peppermint tea can help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the bile ducts, while chicory root tea stimulates bile production and supports liver function. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any herbal tea regimen, especially for those with existing liver conditions or those taking medication.
